items.title: Generation Contract
items.summary: Resolve the product thesis, audience feeling, content spine, assets, and constraints before generating visual directions.
items.intent: Turn an underspecified UI prompt into a compact creative strategy without allowing subject labels or fashionable treatments to choose the aesthetic.
checks: Identify the primary user outcome, audience feeling, and usage context.
Record the required content, strongest available subject evidence, and real asset constraints.
Name the existing or chosen design foundation without choosing a style from category labels alone.
warnings: Do not expose a long design questionnaire when repository evidence can resolve the decisions.
exceptions: A narrowly scoped visual fix may need only a one-line context read.

items.title: Macrostructure First
items.summary: Choose the page or screen structure and reading order before selecting component recipes or detailed styling.
items.intent: Prevent component-first assembly from producing repetitive card grids and weak hierarchy that do not match the user workflow.
checks: Select a macrostructure that matches the primary outcome.
Verify semantic and visual reading order before styling details.
Define how the structure recomposes at narrow widths.
warnings: Do not choose a structure solely because it appears in a reference gallery.
exceptions: A component-only task inherits the surrounding macrostructure.

items.title: Structural Fingerprint
items.summary: Use two stable system constants and one flexible signature so the interface feels authored without coating every region in the same motifs.
items.intent: Create coherence through alignment, type roles, action treatment, media behavior, and rhythm while preventing decorative repetition from becoming a house style.
checks: Name two stable compositional or behavioral constants and one flexible signature.
Use controlled variation instead of identical sections or repeated decoration.
Require a functional or brand reason before repeating one motif across more than two adjacent regions.
warnings: A fingerprint is not a license to apply decoration to every region.
exceptions: Strict platform-native tools may rely primarily on platform structure.

items.title: Spacing Contract
items.summary: Declare page gutters, section gaps, panel insets, stack gaps, and control gaps as separate relationships before component styling.
items.intent: Prevent content from touching borders, sibling items from sticking together, and responsive wrapping from collapsing the visual hierarchy.
checks: Give bordered, tinted, rounded, or elevated surfaces intentional inset on every occupied edge.
Set explicit row and column gaps for sibling stacks, clusters, wraps, and grids.
Use a smaller responsive spacing token instead of collapsing panel inset or sibling gaps to zero.
Inspect longest labels, wrapped copy, validation messages, focus rings, and shadows for edge and sibling clearance.
warnings: Outer margin does not replace panel inset, and child padding does not create a reliable relationship between siblings.
exceptions: Zero gap is valid for an intentional seam such as table cells, segmented controls, tiled media, or a continuous divider.

items.title: Direction Branching
items.summary: Generate three materially different visual routes, then choose the product-derived direction with the strongest audience, content, asset, and responsive fit.
items.intent: Prevent the first category association or most dramatic treatment from becoming the design and create genuine structural variety before implementation.
checks: Make each candidate differ in primary carrier, page silhouette, typography voice, color or material logic, and section rhythm.
Include at least one restrained candidate that does not depend on giant type, fluorescent color, or technical decoration.
Select by audience fit, truthful subject evidence, available assets, content needs, production fit, and responsive viability.
warnings: Do not choose the loudest route merely because it looks most different from a generic template.
exceptions: Strict enterprise tools may prioritize operational clarity over visual drama.

items.title: Visual Move Budget
items.summary: Allocate one display apex, one signature subject or object, one motion motif, one texture or field, and one accent logic across the page.
items.intent: Make the chosen direction feel elegant and coherent by preventing every section from competing with hero-scale typography, decoration, and animation.
checks: Use hero-scale typography at one display apex and step section headings down materially.
Choose one primary visual carrier and one quieter supporting carrier.
Choose one color-deployment mode and one accent family unless supplied brand evidence requires more.
Use one purposeful motion motif instead of universal animation effects.
Rewrite adjacent sections that repeat the same layout family or high-impact move.
warnings: More simultaneous visual moves usually reduce perceived quality instead of increasing ambition.
exceptions: Strictly functional internal tools may need restrained craft rather than expressive art direction.

items.title: Hero Math
items.summary: Constrain display type, vertical budget, subcopy, action position, and mobile proof so expressive heroes remain readable and useful.
items.intent: Prevent ambitious public pages from shipping with clipped title text, accidental line breaks, hidden calls to action, or first screens whose typography consumes the usable height.
checks: Name the intended desktop title line count and mobile title behavior.
Budget fixed chrome, safe spacing, title, subcopy, proof, action, and next-section reveal at a representative short desktop and mobile height.
Give the combined header and hero one viewport-height budget; use one first-screen wrapper or subtract the measured external header height from the hero.
Choose full-viewport coverage, an intentional next-band reveal, or a content-led boundary; do not leave a blank page-background strip below full-bleed media.
Keep subcopy compact enough to scan and within a readable measure.
Keep the first action and primary proof or hook visible early on mobile.
Use responsive type steps and balanced wrapping instead of brittle hard breaks.
warnings: A poster-style hero can be tall, but fixed viewport heights and hidden overflow must not clip its action path, mobile proof, or intended section boundary.
exceptions: Experimental art pages may prioritize poster impact when the user explicitly asks for that direction.

items.title: Image-First Asset Loop
items.summary: When a page needs a memorable visual subject, create or select one focused asset and design around its composition.
items.intent: Move beyond generic atmosphere by making imagery, crop, light, palette, and subject direction shape the layout before detailed styling.
checks: Define the asset role before generating or curating it.
Use concrete subjects such as products, artifacts, places, materials, or output examples instead of generic ambience.
Extract layout decisions from the image crop, light, palette, texture, and negative space.
Make the primary subject dominate or deliberately tension its media crop instead of floating at icon scale inside nested frames.
Keep must-inspect products, faces, labels, and silhouettes inside a crop-safe focal area at wide, short, and narrow aspect ratios.
Do not present generated people, venues, sponsor walls, awards, or screenshots as real evidence.
warnings: Dropping a generated image into a finished generic template rarely improves the design.
exceptions: Strict UI components, dashboards, and text-first documentation may not need imagery.

items.title: Front-Door Art Direction
items.summary: Choose an authored entry strategy, strong visual carrier, memorable type move, navigation posture, and visible motion signature before detailed component styling.
items.intent: Make public interfaces create desire and immediate identity instead of collapsing into a safe specification sheet or generic split hero.
checks: Select an immersive subject field, editorial type composition, artifact tableau, product stage, media-led catalog, kinetic poster, or product-specific custom strategy.
Make the identity, subject, literal offer, and primary action legible in the first viewport.
Treat navigation geometry and mobile behavior as part of the selected composition rather than defaulting to a floating pill.
Choose one visible motion signature tied to the subject or reading route.
warnings: A correct and well-spaced entry can still feel generic when it lacks a strong carrier, crop, type idea, and compositional tension.
exceptions: Dense recurring product tools should prioritize the working surface and may not need expressive front-door art direction.

items.title: Craft Refinement Loop
items.summary: Make one focused visual pass after implementation for optical alignment, spacing, crop, type fit, states, and responsive composition.
items.intent: Polish the selected direction without replacing generation with a scorecard or adding new effects after the system is established.
checks: Resolve optical alignment, display fit, line breaks, spacing, media crop, and interaction states.
Verify the hero ends on an intentional boundary and display copy preserves exact words, spaces, punctuation, and reading order.
Remove repeated display spectacle, decorative rules, fake technical labels, and empty premium space.
Confirm the central subject signal, action, and visual identity survive mobile recomposition.
For commerce, confirm a product, price or plan, meaningful option, and purchase path appear by the first natural scroll.
warnings: Do not introduce a second visual concept during refinement; reroute only when the selected direction itself is wrong.
exceptions: A narrow component fix may not need page-level critique.

items.title: Stable State Geometry
items.summary: Reserve dimensions for controls, rows, media, and async regions so labels and state changes do not shift the layout.
items.intent: Keep interactions predictable and prevent loading, validation, or long content from moving surrounding controls or obscuring information.
checks: Reserve media space with explicit dimensions or aspect ratios.
Test loading, error, success, and longest-label states.
Keep toolbars, rows, counters, and tiles within stable responsive constraints.
warnings: Fixed dimensions must still allow text reflow and zoom.
exceptions: Content-driven editorial blocks may grow naturally when growth does not disrupt interaction.

items.title: Semantic State System
items.summary: Define color, text, icon, and behavior for default, focus, disabled, loading, error, success, and empty states.
items.intent: Make every reachable state legible and recoverable without relying on color alone or inventing inconsistent local treatments.
checks: Provide visible focus and keyboard behavior for every control.
Pair semantic colors with text, icon, or another non-color cue.
Give failure states a clear recovery path.
warnings: Do not disable actions without making the reason discoverable.
exceptions: Unreachable states do not need artificial examples in production UI.

items.title: Mobile Recomposition
items.summary: Change grouping, order, disclosure, and control layout for narrow screens instead of proportionally shrinking desktop UI.
items.intent: Preserve task priority, reading order, accessible targets, and inspectable content under narrow width and limited height.
checks: Define the narrow-screen content and action priority explicitly.
Keep DOM, focus, and visual order coherent.
Test long labels, keyboards, and short landscape height where relevant.
warnings: Do not hide essential context merely to remove horizontal scrolling.
exceptions: Inherently two-dimensional data may use a labeled horizontal scroll region.

items.title: Proportionate UI Review
items.summary: Tie every UI/UX quality claim to design reasoning, static inspection, existing project checks, or rendered review when practical.
items.intent: Prevent unsupported claims without turning ordinary interface work into an automated browser-testing workflow.
checks: Record which UI/UX dimensions, states, and layouts were actually reviewed.
Separate design critique, static inspection, existing project checks, rendered observations, and automated results.
Use Playwright or other e2e tooling only when the user asks, the project already owns that workflow, or the task is site validation.
warnings: A static source review cannot prove every rendered layout or keyboard behavior; label that limitation instead of forcing automation.
exceptions: During early exploration, label quality conclusions as provisional.

items.title: Real Workflow First
items.summary: Implement the primary task or experience as the first screen instead of surrounding an incomplete feature with marketing content.
items.intent: Focus implementation effort on the user outcome and make operational tools usable immediately.
checks: The first screen exposes the core user action or actual subject.
The primary path works through completion or a truthful bounded state.
Promotional framing does not displace the requested tool or experience.
warnings: Do not omit necessary onboarding when the workflow is genuinely unfamiliar or high risk.
exceptions: A user explicitly requesting a campaign or marketing page may need a hero-led first screen.
